title: Glassy-Jelly Festive Brazilian Herbal Bao
description: A unique Brazilian-inspired bao dish featuring a glassy-jelly filling, topped with crispy scallions and a lacquer glaze, all within a gluten-free friendly package.

ingredients.name: gluten-free bao dough
ingredients.notes: use store-bought or make your own

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the gluten-free bao dough, sugar, water, sorghum syrup, cassava flour, cocoa powder, and melted butter. Mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Place the balls onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 15 minutes.
While the bao is baking, prepare the glassy-jelly filling by combining the soy sauce, rice vinegar, grated ginger, and sesame seeds in a small bowl.
Once the bao is done, let it cool for a few minutes before slicing it open.
Fill each bao with the glassy-jelly filling and top with crispy scallions.
Drizzle the lacquer glaze over the bao and serve immediately.
To make the lacquer glaze, combine 1/4 cup of soy sauce, 1/4 cup of rice vinegar, and 2 tablespoons of honey in a small bowl. Heat the mixture in the microwave for 10 seconds and stir until smooth.
